title: Dudhwa National Park
tagline: Dudhwa National Park — wildlife reserve of Uttar Pradesh

overview.short: Dudhwa National Park is a national park in the Terai belt of marshy grasslands in northern Uttar Pradesh, India.
overview.description: Dudhwa National Park is a national park in the Terai belt of marshy grasslands in northern Uttar Pradesh, India. It stretches over an area of 490.3 km2 (189.3 sq mi), with a buffer zone of 190 km2 (73 sq mi). It is part of the Dudhwa Tiger Reserve in the Lakhimpur Kheri district.

topPlaces.name: Dudhwa Tiger Reserve
topPlaces.description: The Dudhwa Tiger Reserve is a protected area in Uttar Pradesh that stretches mainly across the Lakhimpur Kheri and Bahraich districts and comprises the Dudhwa National Park, Kishanpur Wildlife Sanctuary and Katarniaghat Wildlife Sanctuary. It covers an area of 1,284.3 km2 (495.9 sq mi).

items.note: The Dudhwa Tiger Reserve is a protected area in Uttar Pradesh that stretches mainly across the Lakhimpur Kheri and Bahraich districts and comprises the Dudhwa National Park, Kishanpur Wildlife Sanctuary and Katarniaghat Wildlife Sanctuary. It covers an area of 1,284.3 km2 (495.9 sq mi).

faq.q: What is the best time to visit Dudhwa National Park?
faq.a: The best time to visit Dudhwa National Park is Oct – Mar. Summers are 25–42°C and winters are 7–24°C.

faq.q: How do I reach Dudhwa National Park?
faq.a: The nearest airport is Pantnagar Airport (135 km) and the nearest railway station is Bareilly Junction (127 km). Roads are generally good; check seasonal/monsoon conditions before high-altitude or remote stretches.

faq.q: How many days are enough for Dudhwa National Park?
faq.a: 2 days are usually enough to cover the top attractions of Dudhwa National Park at a relaxed pace.

faq.q: How much does a hotel cost in Dudhwa National Park?
faq.a: Stays in Dudhwa National Park start from ₹2,500 per night, with options across 5 price categories.

faq.q: What are the top places to visit in Dudhwa National Park?
faq.a: The top attractions are Dudhwa Tiger Reserve.
